response:
<<Echoes of Time>>

SELECT *
FROM your_table
ORDER BY LENGTH(title) DESC, LENGTH(director) DESC, title, director;

This SQL query organizes the records by sorting them in diminishing order based on the character length of the `title` and `director` columns. In cases where two titles or directors have the same length, the query further sorts them alphabetically by `title` and then by `director` to ensure a deterministic order.

Palindrome: "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
